# Copilot Coding Agent Onboarding — android-design-system

## Overview
Infomaniak Android Design System — a Compose-based UI library providing design tokens and themes. Tokens are generated from Figma via JSON and a Kotlin script. Library modules: `Foundation` (core tokens + `EsdsTheme`) and `PrimitiveTokens` (generated token values). The Gradle build currently includes: `:Foundation`, `:PrimitiveTokens`, `:app` (demo), `:catalog`.

## One-Time Setup
No special setup is required (no git submodules or .env files).

## Local Verification
```bash
./gradlew build                        # compile Foundation, PrimitiveTokens, app, catalog
./gradlew :catalog:testDebugUnitTest   # unit tests (not run by CI)
./gradlew lint                         # lint (not run by CI)
```

> **CI only** runs the token generator workflow (`.github/workflows/generate-android-specific-tokens.yml`). There is no CI build, test, or lint step.

## Token Update Workflow
1. Modify `token-source/android_specific_tokens.json`
2. CI auto-generates code and pushes to the PR branch — **do not manually edit any files generated by** `scripts/generateAndroidSpecificTokens.main.kts` (typically under `PrimitiveTokens/src/` and any `Theme*/src/` folders it creates). Note: the workflow only triggers on changes to `token-source/android_specific_tokens.json` and is skipped for fork PRs (no write access to push back).
3. After CI push, manually expose a public `EsdsTheme.Values` instance for each theme dimension (Light, LightMediumContrast, LightHighContrast, Dark, DarkMediumContrast, DarkHighContrast). The current entry point is:
   `Foundation/src/main/kotlin/com/infomaniak/designsystem/core/defaultvalues/DefaultTheme.kt`
   (If a future `Theme*` module is added to the Gradle build, update it there instead.)

## Project Layout
```
Foundation/             # Core theme infrastructure — EsdsTheme, token interfaces, DefaultTheme
PrimitiveTokens/        # Raw primitive token values (auto-generated Kotlin sources)
app/                    # Demo/sample app
catalog/                # Compose component catalog (has test sources)
build-logic/            # Convention plugins
scripts/generateAndroidSpecificTokens.main.kts   # Token generation script
token-source/android_specific_tokens.json        # Source of truth — edit this, not generated files
```

## PR Review Instructions

- **Never manually edit generated Kotlin sources produced by** `scripts/generateAndroidSpecificTokens.main.kts` (typically under `PrimitiveTokens/src/` and any `Theme*/src/` folders it creates) — they are auto-generated by the CI workflow triggered by changes to `token-source/android_specific_tokens.json`. Other files in `PrimitiveTokens/` (e.g. `build.gradle.kts`) may be edited as needed.
- After token generation, update `DefaultTheme.kt` in `Foundation` (or a future `Theme*` module added to the Gradle build) to expose a public `EsdsTheme.Values` for each dimension: Light, LightMediumContrast, LightHighContrast, Dark, DarkMediumContrast, DarkHighContrast.
- Use only Material3 Compose components (`androidx.compose.material3`).
- When adding/removing a runtime dependency, update `LICENSES.md` at the repo root.
